title: Docker Desktop cannot start if the docker_engine named pipe created by podman before it starts
body:
### Issue Description

I have both Docker Desktop and Podman Desktop installed on the same Windows PC. And from time to time, Docker desktop failed to start because the `docker_engine` named pipe has already been created by Podman:

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/201615/7206ce14-c36e-45a7-b946-4e504f277806)

I looked into the code

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/72ec8824a0112e6ef1466afa95d7f4695aadac01/pkg/machine/wsl/machine.go#L1084-L1087

It seems like we are depending on whether there's a `docker_engine` named pipe already or not. But I guess the problem is, when will the Docker engine start, and when will the Podman engine start, there's no guarantee. If Docker starts first, the named pipe is taken, then it's all good. But if the Podman engine starts first, the name pipe is taken, then the Docker engine is going to fail.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install both Docker Desktop and Podman Desktop on the same PC
2. Reboot a few time, or maybe find a way to adjust when the docker engine starts.
3. As if podman engine starts first, you should see the same error


### Describe the results you received

Docker engine failed to start

### Describe the results you expected

Docker engine should be able to start regardless Podman Desktop installed or not. And there should be an option to disable the `docker_engine` named pipe for podman. Also, if we want to make this automatic, it shouldn't rely on the order of service start and the timing of named pipe acquisition. Maybe check Windows registry see if Docker installed or not instead? 

### podman info output

comments.body:
Podman should not be listening on the docker socket unless it is configured to in Podman Desktop.
@n1hility PTAL

While on Mac we require a helper to be installed which indirectly acts as an enable / disable (with a notification on machine start to do so if not installed), on Windows it just happens as part of machine start. I agree we should add an option to disable/enable it, which desktop can call as part of compatibilty mode.

> It seems like we are depending on whether there's a docker_engine named pipe already or not. But I guess the problem 
> is, when will the Docker engine start, and when will the Podman engine start, there's no guarantee. If Docker starts first, 
> the named pipe is taken, then it's all good. But if the Podman engine starts first, the name pipe is taken, then
> the Docker  engine is going to fail.

The detection and backoff in the Windows case isn't there to coordinate between the two, its just to allow the machine start to fallback inform the user of the location to set DOCKER_HOST (as part of a CLI start) when the global default is not available.

After shutting down Podman machine and Podman Desktop still seeing the same error

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/201615/6e9dec4f-776d-44ca-9307-3155244dbcb0)

It turns out `win-sshproxy.exe` is still running and holding a handle to the named pipe, and that's probably why I see an access denied error. After killing the ssh proxy process, now my docker can start normally.

FYI for people who run into the same issue, a temporary solution is to:

1. Shutdown podman machine & desktop
2. Kill all win-sshproxy.exe which still holds a handle to the `docker_engine` named pipe (you can find it from Resource Monitor)
3. Start Docker desktop and it should works normally
4. Start Podman machine & desktop and it should works normally

> It turns out `win-sshproxy.exe` is still running and holding a handle to the named pipe, and that's probably why I see an access denied error. After killing the ssh proxy process, now my docker can start normally.

ah, Any chance you manually killed the podman machine (e.g. via a wsl --terminate or sending a kill to a wsl process)? A normal clean shutdown (podman machine stop) should stop the win-sshproxy process.

I recall I saw some error message when running `podman machine stop`. Forgot what I saw, but it seems like the shutdown process wasn't done successfully, maybe that's why the ssh proxy was stilling running. If I can find some time may try again later today see if I can see what's the error I saw.

> I recall I saw some error message when running `podman machine stop`. Forgot what I saw, but it seems like the shutdown process wasn't done successfully, maybe that's why the ssh proxy was stilling running. If I can find some time may try again later today see if I can see what's the error I saw.

ah yes that would do it. thanks for offering to report back, if you happen to see that again would be curious what you saw.

okay, I just rebooted and ran into the same problem

Looks like machine stop shows it's stopped successfully. But somehow the win-sshproxy.exe process is still there and holding the `docker_engine` name pipe

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/201615/c7f8bd42-24cd-4065-b75e-a87588cfdc4f)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
